name: Wailupe
desc: Tiny peninsula community of roughly 140 homes between Aina Haina and Niu Valley — quiet, tucked away, waterfront charm.
insider: With roughly 140 homes on a small peninsula, this is one of the most tight-knit communities in East Honolulu — everyone genuinely knows everyone
The Wailupe shoreline is a rocky pocket beach that only neighbors access — it's not a swimming beach but the tidepools and sunset views are fantastic
Wailupe Circle and Wailupe Place form the entire street network — you can walk the whole neighborhood in about 15 minutes
Location between Aina Haina and Niu Valley means Aina Haina Shopping Center is a 3-minute drive west and Niu Valley Shopping Center is 3 minutes east
The peninsula sits right on Kalanianaole Highway, so front-facing homes get road noise — the interior homes are significantly quieter
Homes here rarely come on the market — maybe 2-3 per year — and when they do, word often spreads through the neighborhood before MLS listing
The shoreline area is vulnerable to high surf and king tides in winter — oceanfront homeowners should understand their flood zone designation
There are no HOA fees or gated access — it's an open, unpretentious neighborhood that feels more like a rural cul-de-sac than suburban Honolulu
The Niu Valley community often includes Wailupe in its events and neighborhood watch — the two communities function almost as one
Street parking is limited since most homes have narrow driveways — if you're visiting, park on Kalanianaole Highway and walk in
